summary: c=2 native batch full-attention value-scan specialization: long-context Qwen rows split value accumulation across thread groups, improving context-kernel time and unprofiled c2 decode while retaining correctness. c2 remains below c1, so no retained c2 throughput claim is made.

software.hipcc_version:
HIP version: 7.2.53211-364a905
AMD clang version 22.0.0git (/srcdest/rocm-llvm f58b06dce1f9c15707c5f808fd002e18c2accf7e)
Target: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
InstalledDir: /opt/rocm/lib/llvm/bin

code_change.description: qwen35_paged_full_attn_decode_context_tensor_batch_kernel now uses shared partials to split value-cache accumulation across multiple thread groups for 1024-thread, long-context Qwen-shaped c2 decode; tiny primitive fixtures keep the old c1-style accumulation path.

profiler.raw_trace_not_retained_reason: raw rocprof CSV is large and intentionally left under /tmp; this compact artifact records aggregate evidence only

conclusion.reason: Value-scan specialization materially reduces the native batch full-attention context kernel to 0.588x of the prior launch-shape artifact and improves c2 projection-check aggregate decode to 1.054x of that prior, but c2 aggregate remains only 0.838x c1, so retained c2 promotion remains blocked.

commands.primary_verify: active loop verify command after prebuilding commit 788670a6 cache with HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1; output /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-512-128.json
